raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.3,"RX.2":0.3,"RX.3":0.3,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Which type of feedback are you looking for on this paper draft: (1) assessment of conceptual novelty and logical consistency, (2) suggestions for structure and writing polish, or (3) a comprehensive review covering multiple aspects?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The vague delegation 'What do you think?' likely expects feedback on the core idea and contribution, making conceptual reconstruction a central responsibility.","R1.2":"General thoughts on a research draft typically include checking if the argument holds together, positioning logical consistency as central.","R1.3":"Feedback would naturally verify if reported results support the claims, rendering evidence-claim alignment central to the projection.","R1.4":"Assessing the claimed novelty against prior work is a key part of opining on a research artifact, making this dimension central.","R1.6":"Opinions on a draft often cover readability and clarity at the sentence level, establishing writing polish as a central expectation.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is a peripheral nice-to-have in general feedback to ensure responsible representation of the work's limitations.","RX.2":"Avoiding overclaims is peripherally relevant when providing thoughts to help refine exaggerated statements in the draft.","RX.3":"Adherence to the stated scope is a peripheral consideration in feedback to confirm the artifact stays within its intended bounds.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is peripherally included to flag any potential misuse risks in the described translation framework.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are peripheral in this context to verify accurate attribution of methods and baselines."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Which type of feedback are you looking for on this paper draft: (1) assessment of conceptual novelty and logical consistency, (2) suggestions for structure and writing polish, or (3) a comprehensive review covering multiple aspects?

rationale.R1.1: The vague delegation 'What do you think?' likely expects feedback on the core idea and contribution, making conceptual reconstruction a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.2: General thoughts on a research draft typically include checking if the argument holds together, positioning logical consistency as central.
rationale.R1.3: Feedback would naturally verify if reported results support the claims, rendering evidence-claim alignment central to the projection.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the claimed novelty against prior work is a key part of opining on a research artifact, making this dimension central.
rationale.R1.6: Opinions on a draft often cover readability and clarity at the sentence level, establishing writing polish as a central expectation.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is a peripheral nice-to-have in general feedback to ensure responsible representation of the work's limitations.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overclaims is peripherally relevant when providing thoughts to help refine exaggerated statements in the draft.
rationale.RX.3: Adherence to the stated scope is a peripheral consideration in feedback to confirm the artifact stays within its intended bounds.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is peripherally included to flag any potential misuse risks in the described translation framework.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are peripheral in this context to verify accurate attribution of methods and baselines.
